summary: Russian FPV operators in the Sumy region are targeting Ukrainian 2S22 "Bogdan" self-propelled artillery with fiber-optic guided "KVN" drones. Ukrainian forces in the 147th Artillery Brigade are maintaining a full-cycle drone workshop for repairs, firmware updates, and custom modifications. The 35th Marine Brigade's "KATRAN" battalion is conducting FPV strikes on Russian positions, emphasizing that no distance is safe for the enemy. Russian forces are using the "Yolka" anti-drone system to kinetically intercept Ukrainian "Hornet" fixed-wing UAVs, causing warhead detonations. The "Rubicon" Center reported 90 fixed-wing UAV kills on April 9 and 173 on April 8, along with personnel losses and cooperation with Russian Aerospace Forces. A Ukrainian "Lancet" loitering munition struck an AFV after hitting an FPV drone lens during an ambush. The Ukrainian firm XDOWN unveiled the STUD portable drone, capable of 300 km/h speeds, 64 km range, and 800g warheads, with foldable design. Ukrainian forces are experiencing reduced FPV control frequency and increased REB antenna sizes. In the Kostiantynivka direction, Ukrainian 36th Marine Brigade soldiers survived a Russian FPV attack after detecting it with a drone detector.
